item.title: 如何使用异步操作跟踪 setState 之后的状态？ | How to track state after setState with async actions?
item.description:
杨宇镇

发表于 4 月 15 日

要知道， useState函数是异步的。
所以默认情况下，如果您调用setState函数并尝试获取值，您将获得未更改的状态。
此外，即使useState函数是异步的，它也不提供Promise对象。
因此，尝试对状态更改进行异步处理令人头疼。

是的。 您应该调用“useEffect”函数并使您的回调函数在函数组件的状态更改后执行某些操作。

不过别担心！ 我有一个好主意。 让我们看看如何解决它。

准备一个具有Promise的Ref对象。

 const [getState, setState] = useState('') const promiseRef = useRef()

包装setState函数并返回Promise 。

 const setStatePromise = value => { setState(value); // your original set state function return new Promise(resolve => promiseRef.current = resolve) // Dispose when promise resolved. .then(() => { promiseRef.current = undefined }) }

调用useEffect并解析委托的Promise 。 不要忘记添加状态依赖以使Promise的 fulfilled 状态出乎意料。

 useEffect(() => { ref.current && ref.current() }, [getState]) // only for your specified state.

使用包装的设置状态函数并像往常一样进行异步工作！

 const handleSubmit = async e => { await setStatePromise('CHANGED') // do something more const response = await fetch('/path/to/submit', { method: 'POST', body: `myState=${getState}` // it will get changed state such as 'CHANGED'! }) const json = await response.json() // do feedback! }

让我们看看它是如何工作的：

  1. 准备一个ref对象来保存委托Promise的resolve函数。
  2. 创建一个包装setState函数的函数。 它的功能将使用现有的setState函数创建一个新的Promise对象，并将它的resolve函数加载到代理ref对象中。
  3. 在useEffect回调中，调用委托中加载的resolve函数使Promise状态 fulfilled。 状态更改后，您可以在等待下一个异步操作的Promise函数后获得更改后的状态。
  4. 在 wrapped 函数中，我还添加了then callback for perform delete the delegated resolve function，它处理了一个已经被 resolve 的Promise.resolve 。 此清理应减少内存泄漏。

这么棘手吧？
快乐的反应！

享受！

一旦暂停，组合将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

取消暂停后，composite 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，所有复合帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果复合没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记复合材料的操作：

降低所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging composite will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagging composite 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ukjin Yang

Posted on Apr 15

You know, the useState function is asynchronous.
So by default, if you call the setState function and try to get value, you will get the unchanged state.
Furthermore, even though the useState function is asynchronous, it doesn't provide a Promise object.
So trying to do asynchronous works on state changes is making a headache.

Yes. you should call the 'useEffect' function and make your callback function to do something after state change in your function component.

But don't worry! I have a good idea. let's check it out how to solve it.

Prepare a Ref object that have a Promise. 

const [getState, setState] = useState('')
const promiseRef = useRef()

Wrap setState function and return Promise. 

const setStatePromise = value => { setState(value); // your original set state function return new Promise(resolve => promiseRef.current = resolve) // Dispose when promise resolved. .then(() => { promiseRef.current = undefined })
}

Call useEffect and make resolve the delegated Promise. Don't forget add state dependency to make unexpected Promise's fulfilled state. 

useEffect(() => { ref.current && ref.current()
}, [getState]) // only for your specified state.

Use wrapped set state function and do your async work as normally! 

const handleSubmit = async e => { await setStatePromise('CHANGED') // do something more const response = await fetch('/path/to/submit', { method: 'POST', body: `myState=${getState}` // it will get changed state such as 'CHANGED'! }) const json = await response.json() // do feedback!
}

Let's see How it works:

  1. Prepare a ref object to hold the delegate Promise's resolve function.
  2. Create a function that wraps the setState function. Its features will create a new Promise object with the existing setState function, and the resolve function from it will be loaded into the surrogate ref object.
  3. In the useEffect callback, call the resolve function loaded in the delegate to make the Promise state fulfilled. After state changes, you will can get a changed state after awaited Promise function for the next async action.
  4. In wrapped function, I also added then callback for perform delete the delegated resolve function, which disposes a Promise.resolve that has already been resolved. This cleanup should reduce memory leaks.

So tricky, huh?
Happy react-ing!

P.S. I made typescript function that do same thing as above. enjoy!

Once suspended, composite will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, composite will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by composite will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If composite is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag composite:

Make all posts by composite less visible

composite consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging composite will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: composite

item.title: 阿蒙认知 | Amaon cognito
item.description:
萨西什 P

发表于 4 月 15 日

为用户提供与 Web 或移动应用程序交互的身份

所有用户的登录功能

  • 与 API 网关和应用程序负载均衡器集成

    • 为用户提供临时 AWS 凭证，以便他们可以直接访问 AWS 资源

  • 作为身份提供者与 Cognito 用户池集成

    • 特征：

    • 为您的 Web 和移动应用程序创建无服务器用户数据库
    • 简单登录：用户名（或电子邮件）/密码组合
    • 重设密码
    • 电子邮件和电话号码验证
    • 艺术硕士
    • 联合身份：来自 facebook、google、SAML 的用户
    • 功能：如果凭据在其他地方遭到破坏，则阻止用户
    • 登录发回一个 JSON Web 令牌 (JWT) Daigram：

    一旦暂停，sathish3sank 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，sathish3sank 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，sathish3sank 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 sathish3sank 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 sathish3sank：

    降低 sathish3sank 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 sathish3sank 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Sathish P

    Posted on Apr 15

    What does Cognito? Give users an identity to interact with web or mobile application

    Cognito user pools:

    • sign in functionality for all users
    • integrate with API gateway & application load balancer

    Cognito identity pools(Federated Identity):

    • provides temporary AWS credentials to users so they can access AWS resources directly
    • integrate with cognito user pools as an identity provider

    Features:

    • create a serverless database of user for your web and mobile apps
    • simple login:username(or email)/password combination
    • password reset
    • Email and phone number verification
    • MFA
    • federated identities: users from facebook,google,SAML
    • feature:block users if credentials are compromised elsewhere
    • login sends back a JSON Web Token(JWT) Daigram:

    Once suspended, sathish3sank will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, sathish3sank will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by sathish3sank will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If sathish3sank is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag sathish3sank:

    Make all posts by sathish3sank less visible

    sathish3sank consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging sathish3sank will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: sathish3sank

item.title: 使用 ClojureScript REPL 增强您的 JS/TS 项目 | Supercharge Your JS/TS Project with ClojureScript REPL
item.description:
如果还没有，请先在您的机器上安装Java SDK ，以及节点和pnpm本身。

你可能有现有的项目，你可以增压。 作为示例，我们首先创建全新的Vite项目。

 $ mkdir -p /tmp/hi && cd /tmp/hi $ pnpm create vite@latest . --template react-ts

我们现在有一个工作项目，使用例如pnpm run build && pnpm exec vite preview --open进行验证。

现在，添加shadow-cljs 。

 $ pnpm install --save-dev shadow-cljs $ pnpm exec shadow-cljs init $ pnpm exec shadow-cljs info

最后一个命令将花费一些时间来获取所有必需的依赖项。 请耐心等待，在此之后，如果所有安装都正确，那么所有的疑虑都会得到解决。

如果你的结果或多或少像下面这样，那么恭喜你，你已经迈出了开悟的第一步。

 shadow-cljs - config: /private/tmp/x3/shadow-cljs.edn === Version jar: 2.22.10 cli: 2.22.10 deps: 1.3.4 config-version: 2.22.10 === Paths cli: /private/tmp/x3/node_modules/.pnpm/shadow-cljs@2.22.10/node_modules/shadow-cljs/cli/dist.js config: /private/tmp/x3/shadow-cljs.edn project: /private/tmp/x3 cache: .shadow-cljs === Java openjdk version "19.0.2" 2023-01-17 OpenJDK Runtime Environment Homebrew (build 19.0.2) OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Homebrew (build 19.0.2, mixed mode, sharing) === Source Paths /private/tmp/x3/src/dev /private/tmp/x3/src/main /private/tmp/x3/src/test ... === Dependencies shadow-cljs - updating dependencies Retrieving... ... ... ... shadow-cljs - dependencies updated [thheller/shadow-cljs "2.22.10" :classifier "aot"] [com.bhauman/cljs-test-display "0.1.1"] [cider/piggieback "0.5.3" :exclusions [[org.clojure/clojure] [org.clojure/clojurescript] [nrepl]]] [io.methvin/directory-watcher "0.17.1"] [net.java.dev.jna/jna "5.12.1"] [org.slf4j/slf4j-api "1.7.36"] [org.clojure/clojure "1.11.1"] [org.clojure/core.specs.alpha "0.2.62"] [org.clojure/spec.alpha "0.3.218"] [com.cognitect/transit-cljs "0.8.280"] [com.cognitect/transit-js "0.8.874"] [org.clojure/google-closure-library "0.0-20230227-c7c0a541"] [thheller/shadow-cljsjs "0.0.22"] [org.clojure/core.async "1.5.648"] [org.clojure/tools.analyzer.jvm "1.2.2"] [org.clojure/core.memoize "1.0.253"] [org.clojure/core.cache "1.0.225"] [org.clojure/data.priority-map "1.1.0"] [org.clojure/tools.analyzer "1.1.0"] [org.ow2.asm/asm "9.2"] [com.google.javascript/closure-compiler-unshaded "v20230228"] [com.google.code.gson/gson "2.9.1"] [com.google.guava/guava "31.0.1-jre"] [com.google.code.findbugs/jsr305 "3.0.2"] [com.google.guava/listenablefuture "9999.0-empty-to-avoid-conflict-with-guava"] [com.google.j2objc/j2objc-annotations "1.3"] [org.checkerframework/checker-qual "3.12.0"] [com.google.guava/failureaccess "1.0.1"] [org.jspecify/jspecify "0.2.0"] [com.google.auto.value/auto-value-annotations "1.6"] [javax.annotation/jsr250-api "1.0"] [args4j "2.33"] [com.google.errorprone/error_prone_annotations "2.15.0"] [com.google.protobuf/protobuf-java "3.21.12"] [org.apache.ant/ant "1.10.11"] [org.apache.ant/ant-launcher "1.10.11"] [com.google.re2j/re2j "1.3"] [ring/ring-core "1.9.6" :exclusions [[clj-time]]] [commons-fileupload "1.4"] [commons-io "2.11.0"] [crypto-equality "1.0.1"] [crypto-random "1.2.1"] [commons-codec "1.15"] [ring/ring-codec "1.2.0"] [hiccup "1.0.5"] [thheller/shadow-undertow "0.3.1"] [io.undertow/undertow-core "2.2.4.Final"] [org.jboss.logging/jboss-logging "3.4.1.Final"] [org.jboss.threads/jboss-threads "3.1.0.Final" :exclusions [[org.wildfly.common/wildfly-common]]] [org.jboss.xnio/xnio-api "3.8.0.Final" :exclusions [[org.jboss.threads/jboss-threads]]] [org.wildfly.client/wildfly-client-config "1.0.1.Final"] [org.wildfly.common/wildfly-common "1.5.2.Final"] [org.jboss.xnio/xnio-nio "3.8.0.Final" :scope "runtime" :exclusions [[org.wildfly.common/wildfly-common]]] [thheller/shadow-client "1.3.3"] [fipp "0.6.26"] [org.clojure/core.rrb-vector "0.1.2"] [org.clojure/google-closure-library-third-party "0.0-20230227-c7c0a541"] [org.clojure/clojurescript "1.11.60" :exclusions [[com.google.javascript/closure-compiler-unshaded] [org.clojure/google-closure-library] [org.clojure/google-closure-library-third-party]]] [org.clojure/tools.reader "1.3.6"] [org.clojure/tools.cli "1.0.206"] [org.clojure/data.json "2.4.0"] [nrepl "1.0.0"] [com.cognitect/transit-clj "1.0.329"] [com.cognitect/transit-java "1.0.362"] [com.fasterxml.jackson.core/jackson-core "2.8.7"] [javax.xml.bind/jaxb-api "2.3.0"] [org.msgpack/msgpack "0.6.12"] [com.googlecode.json-simple/json-simple "1.1.1" :exclusions [[junit]]] [org.javassist/javassist "3.18.1-GA"] [expound "0.9.0"] [thheller/shadow-util "0.7.0"]

哦，好吧，请不要感到害怕。 如果与 npm node_modules 中的所有依赖项（例如pnpm ls --depth 15 ）进行比较，这些 Clojure 依赖项要简单得多。

好的，现在，我假设你已经理解并完成了 JS 或 TypeScript 部分，它和往常一样。

现在，增压部分是，我们如何使用 Clojurescript REPL 来开始启蒙。

 pnpm exec shadow-cljs browser-repl

如果一切顺利，我们将受到 cljs repl 以及一个打开的浏览器窗口的欢迎，我们对 repl 进行的每一次评估都将在此浏览器窗口中执行。

 shadow-cljs - config: /private/tmp/x3/shadow-cljs.edn shadow-cljs - server version: 2.22.10 running at http://localhost:9630 shadow-cljs - nREPL server started on port 65047 [:browser-repl] Configuring build. [:browser-repl] Compiling ... [:browser-repl] Build completed. (119 files, 118 compiled, 0 warnings, 10.19s) cljs.user=>

这个 repl 与浏览器控制台的目的几乎相同，我们可以在其中键入有效的表达式来快速检查结果。 然而，这或多或少是一个强大的LISP REPL而不是 JavaScript 控制台。

有了这个 repl，我们可以实时做任何允许的事情，与实时运行时交互，调用所有可用的 JS api 以及实时修改它的每个方面，——无需重启、重新加载或类似的东西。

我们将使用我们自己可爱的编辑器（例如Emacs或VSCode）与此 repl 进行交互，从而获得最佳体验。 如果你刚开始，我建议设置Calva ，入门指南对新手非常友好。

对于这篇文章，我们将简单地与命令行 repl 交互以展示一些演示。

将此粘贴到 repl 中：

 (ns my.app (:require ["react" :as react]))

现在名为my.app的namespace已创建，我们的活动命名空间已从cljs.user移动到my.app ，如提示所示。

这个命名空间现在可以通过名为react的符号访问 react js 模块。 在称为interning的名称空间中将符号绑定到值的过程。

让我们尝试安装新的 npm 包，例如uid 。 一个小巧（130B 到 205B）且快速的实用程序，用于随机化固定长度的唯一 ID。

打开新的终端外壳并执行：

 $ pnpm install --save uid

回到我们的 cljs repl，我们现在可以更新my.app ns 来实习这个新安装的库。

 (ns my.app (:require ["react" :as react] ["uid" :as uid]))

现在我们可以访问uid模块，我们可以调用uid模块提供的uid函数，如下所示：

 ;; call uid function (uid/uid) ;; call it 100 times, return a list of 100 random uid (repeatedly 100 #(uid/uid))

接下来，我们定义一个名为new-rand-div函数，它将返回一个新的 HTMLDivElement，其中包含一个随机的 uid 字符串。

 (defn new-rand-div [] (let [x (. js/document createElement "div")] (set! (.-innerText x) (uid/uid)) x))

并测试调用以验证。

现在，让我们激活在 repl 启动期间打开的浏览器窗口，如果您检查该窗口，它有一个名为“app”的 div 元素。 我们将修改 dom 以展示我们如何与运行时交互以轻松修改页面。

 (let [x (. js/document getElementById "app")] (dotimes [i 100] (. x append (new-rand-div))))

验证浏览器窗口现在是否显示这 100 个随机 uid。

好吧，就到此为止。 我希望这足以证明我们是如何被增压的，即使只是一点点。

很好，节省一些资源，我们现在应该退出 repl 来停止它。

感谢您的阅读，如果您需要更多此类内容，请告诉我，我会抽出一些时间来创建后续帖子，这应该很有趣。



原文:

If not yet, first install the Java SDK on your machine, as well as node and pnpm itself.

You may have existing project that you can supercharge. As an example, here we start by creating brand new Vite project.

$ mkdir -p /tmp/hi && cd /tmp/hi
$ pnpm create vite@latest . --template react-ts

We now have a working project, verify with e.g. pnpm run build && pnpm exec vite preview --open.

Now, add shadow-cljs.

$ pnpm install --save-dev shadow-cljs
$ pnpm exec shadow-cljs init
$ pnpm exec shadow-cljs info

The last command will take some time while grabbing all the required dependencies. Be patient, after this, if all the installation goes right, all the doubts will be paid off.

If your result is looks more or less like below, then congratulations, you have taken the very first step towards enlightenment.

shadow-cljs - config: /private/tmp/x3/shadow-cljs.edn
=== Version
jar:            2.22.10
cli:            2.22.10
deps:           1.3.4
config-version: 2.22.10

=== Paths
cli:     /private/tmp/x3/node_modules/.pnpm/shadow-cljs@2.22.10/node_modules/shadow-cljs/cli/dist.js
config:  /private/tmp/x3/shadow-cljs.edn
project: /private/tmp/x3
cache:   .shadow-cljs

=== Java
openjdk version "19.0.2" 2023-01-17
OpenJDK Runtime Environment Homebrew (build 19.0.2)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Homebrew (build 19.0.2, mixed mode, sharing)

=== Source Paths
/private/tmp/x3/src/dev
/private/tmp/x3/src/main
/private/tmp/x3/src/test

...
=== Dependencies
shadow-cljs - updating dependencies
Retrieving...

...
...

...
shadow-cljs - dependencies updated
[thheller/shadow-cljs "2.22.10" :classifier "aot"]
[com.bhauman/cljs-test-display "0.1.1"]
[cider/piggieback "0.5.3" :exclusions [[org.clojure/clojure] [org.clojure/clojurescript] [nrepl]]]
[io.methvin/directory-watcher "0.17.1"]
  [net.java.dev.jna/jna "5.12.1"]
  [org.slf4j/slf4j-api "1.7.36"]
[org.clojure/clojure "1.11.1"]
  [org.clojure/core.specs.alpha "0.2.62"]
  [org.clojure/spec.alpha "0.3.218"]
[com.cognitect/transit-cljs "0.8.280"]
  [com.cognitect/transit-js "0.8.874"]
[org.clojure/google-closure-library "0.0-20230227-c7c0a541"]
[thheller/shadow-cljsjs "0.0.22"]
[org.clojure/core.async "1.5.648"]
  [org.clojure/tools.analyzer.jvm "1.2.2"]
    [org.clojure/core.memoize "1.0.253"]
      [org.clojure/core.cache "1.0.225"]
        [org.clojure/data.priority-map "1.1.0"]
    [org.clojure/tools.analyzer "1.1.0"]
    [org.ow2.asm/asm "9.2"]
[com.google.javascript/closure-compiler-unshaded "v20230228"]
  [com.google.code.gson/gson "2.9.1"]
  [com.google.guava/guava "31.0.1-jre"]
    [com.google.code.findbugs/jsr305 "3.0.2"]
    [com.google.guava/listenablefuture "9999.0-empty-to-avoid-conflict-with-guava"]
    [com.google.j2objc/j2objc-annotations "1.3"]
    [org.checkerframework/checker-qual "3.12.0"]
  [com.google.guava/failureaccess "1.0.1"]
  [org.jspecify/jspecify "0.2.0"]
  [com.google.auto.value/auto-value-annotations "1.6"]
  [javax.annotation/jsr250-api "1.0"]
  [args4j "2.33"]
  [com.google.errorprone/error_prone_annotations "2.15.0"]
  [com.google.protobuf/protobuf-java "3.21.12"]
  [org.apache.ant/ant "1.10.11"]
    [org.apache.ant/ant-launcher "1.10.11"]
  [com.google.re2j/re2j "1.3"]
[ring/ring-core "1.9.6" :exclusions [[clj-time]]]
  [commons-fileupload "1.4"]
  [commons-io "2.11.0"]
  [crypto-equality "1.0.1"]
  [crypto-random "1.2.1"]
    [commons-codec "1.15"]
  [ring/ring-codec "1.2.0"]
[hiccup "1.0.5"]
[thheller/shadow-undertow "0.3.1"]
  [io.undertow/undertow-core "2.2.4.Final"]
    [org.jboss.logging/jboss-logging "3.4.1.Final"]
    [org.jboss.threads/jboss-threads "3.1.0.Final" :exclusions [[org.wildfly.common/wildfly-common]]]
    [org.jboss.xnio/xnio-api "3.8.0.Final" :exclusions [[org.jboss.threads/jboss-threads]]]
      [org.wildfly.client/wildfly-client-config "1.0.1.Final"]
      [org.wildfly.common/wildfly-common "1.5.2.Final"]
    [org.jboss.xnio/xnio-nio "3.8.0.Final" :scope "runtime" :exclusions [[org.wildfly.common/wildfly-common]]]
[thheller/shadow-client "1.3.3"]
[fipp "0.6.26"]
  [org.clojure/core.rrb-vector "0.1.2"]
[org.clojure/google-closure-library-third-party "0.0-20230227-c7c0a541"]
[org.clojure/clojurescript "1.11.60" :exclusions [[com.google.javascript/closure-compiler-unshaded] [org.clojure/google-closure-library] [org.clojure/google-closure-library-third-party]]]
[org.clojure/tools.reader "1.3.6"]
[org.clojure/tools.cli "1.0.206"]
[org.clojure/data.json "2.4.0"]
[nrepl "1.0.0"]
[com.cognitect/transit-clj "1.0.329"]
  [com.cognitect/transit-java "1.0.362"]
    [com.fasterxml.jackson.core/jackson-core "2.8.7"]
    [javax.xml.bind/jaxb-api "2.3.0"]
    [org.msgpack/msgpack "0.6.12"]
      [com.googlecode.json-simple/json-simple "1.1.1" :exclusions [[junit]]]
      [org.javassist/javassist "3.18.1-GA"]
[expound "0.9.0"]
[thheller/shadow-util "0.7.0"]

Oh well, please don't feel intimidated. Those Clojure dependencies is much much more simple if you compare with all those in npm node_modules (e.g. pnpm ls --depth 15).

Ok, for now, i assume you already understand and done with the JS or TypeScript part, its the same business as usual.

Now, the supercharge part is, how we can have Clojurescript REPL to begin the enlightenment.

pnpm exec shadow-cljs browser-repl

If all goes well, we'll be welcomed by the cljs repl as well as an open browser window where every evaluation we put on the repl will be executed in this browser window.

shadow-cljs - config: /private/tmp/x3/shadow-cljs.edn
shadow-cljs - server version: 2.22.10 running at http://localhost:9630
shadow-cljs - nREPL server started on port 65047
[:browser-repl] Configuring build.
[:browser-repl] Compiling ...
[:browser-repl] Build completed. (119 files, 118 compiled, 0 warnings, 10.19s)
cljs.user=>

This repl is pretty much has the same purpose like the browser console, where we can type a valid expression to quickly check the result. However, this is more or less of a powerful LISP REPL instead of JavaScript console.

With this repl, we can do anything allowed in real time, interacting with the live runtime, call all available JS apis as well as modifying every aspect of it live, -- without restart, reload or anything like that.

We'll get the best experience interacting with this repl with our own lovely editor, e.g. Emacs or VSCode. If you just starting, i suggest to setup Calva, the get started guide is so newbie friendly.

For this post, we'll simply interact with the command line repl to show some demonstration.

Paste this in the repl:

(ns my.app (:require ["react" :as react]))

Now the namespace named my.app has been created and our active namespace has moved from cljs.user to my.app, as shown by the prompt.

This namespace has now have access to react js module via a symbol named react. The process of binding symbol to value within a namespace called as interning.

Lets try install new npm package, e.g. uid. A tiny (130B to 205B) and fast utility to randomize unique IDs of fixed length.

Open new terminal shell and do:

$ pnpm install --save uid

Back to our cljs repl, we now can update the my.app ns to intern this new installed library.

(ns my.app (:require ["react" :as react] ["uid" :as uid]))

Now that we have access to uid module, we can call the uid function provided by the uid module, like so:

;; call uid function
(uid/uid) ;; call it 100 times, return a list of 100 random uid
(repeatedly 100 #(uid/uid))

Next, we define a function named new-rand-div, it will return new HTMLDivElement with a random uid string inside.

(defn new-rand-div [] (let [x (. js/document createElement "div")] (set! (.-innerText x) (uid/uid)) x))

And test calling to verify.

Now, lets activate the browser window that is opened during the start of the repl, if you inspect that window, it has div element named "app". We will modify the dom to show how we interact with the runtime to modify the page with ease.

(let [x (. js/document getElementById "app")] (dotimes [i 100] (. x append (new-rand-div))))

Verify that the browser window now displays those 100 random uids.

Okay, that is for now. I hope it is enough to convince how we have been supercharged, even if only just by a litle.

Be nice, save some resource, we should now quit the repl to stop it.

Thank you for reading, let me know if you need more of this kinds, i'll spare some time to create follow up posts, which should be moar interesting.
item.author: reedho
